mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly inflation above 2% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns exceed 1.5% in 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2% monthly, or Rank IC > 0.05 in inflationary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and declining real savings (RSAFS) signals robust corporate profitability and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of periods where INDPRO and RSAFS both rise, or pairwise correlation between proxies drops below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 100k signals weak labor market dynamics, reducing consumer spending and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns exceed 1% in 40%+ of instances where PAYEMS declines below 100k, or t-test p > 0.1 for negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) above 1.8M units indicate strong demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 50%+ of periods where HOUST exceeds 1.8M, or Rank IC < 0.02 in construction-driven regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in GDP (GDPC1) below 2% growth signals economic slowdown, reducing corporate earnings expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns exceed 2% in 50%+ of instances where GDPC1 declines below 2%, or t-test p > 0.05 for negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL indicate accommodative policy amid inflation, creating a 'higher for longer' rate environment that drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 50%+ of periods where FEDFUNDS < 3% and CPIAUCSL > 2%, or interaction effect size < 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 115 signals manufacturing weakness, reducing corporate margins and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns exceed 0.8% in 40%+ of instances where INDPRO falls below 115, or Rank IC > 0.03 in contractionary regimes.
